questions.question: Explain the concept of packet switching and how it differs from circuit switching.
keyPoints: Definition of packet switching as breaking data into independent packets
Each packet travels independently across network
No dedicated path required unlike circuit switching
Resources are shared among multiple users
More efficient for bursty traffic

questions.question: Describe datagram packet switching with its advantages and disadvantages.
keyPoints: Connectionless approach - each packet independent
Packets may take different routes to destination
No setup phase required before transmission
Advantages: resilience to failures, flexibility
Disadvantages: unpredictable delivery order, potential for out-of-sequence delivery

questions.question: Explain virtual circuit packet switching and how it establishes communication.
keyPoints: Connection-oriented approach with setup phase
Logical path established before data transfer
All packets follow the same route
Uses Virtual Circuit Identifier (VCI) in header
Similar to telephone call establishment

questions.question: List and explain the different types of delays experienced in packet-switched networks.
keyPoints: Processing delay: time to examine and route packet
Queuing delay: waiting time in router buffers
Transmission delay: time to push packet onto link
Propagation delay: time for signal to travel through medium
Total delay is sum of all these components

questions.question: What is store-and-forward technique in packet switching? Explain its significance.
keyPoints: Router receives entire packet before forwarding
Complete error checking performed at each hop
Packet stored in memory temporarily
Ensures reliability but adds delay
Prevents propagation of corrupted packets

questions.question: Compare datagram switching and virtual circuit switching on any five parameters.
keyPoints: Connection type: connectionless vs connection-oriented
Path: variable vs fixed
Setup phase: not required vs required
Routing: per-packet vs per-session
Reliability: less reliable vs more reliable
Delay: variable vs predictable

questions.question: Explain why packet switching is more suitable for modern data networks than circuit switching.
keyPoints: Efficient bandwidth utilization through sharing
Better handling of bursty traffic patterns
Scalability to accommodate more users
Cost-effective implementation
Supports diverse applications and services

questions.question: Explain the operation of packet switching in detail with a neat diagram showing the path from source to destination through multiple routers. Include the structure of a packet and explain each component.
keyPoints: Diagram showing source → Router1 → Router2 → Router3 → Destination
Packet structure: Header (addresses, sequence, VCI) + Data + Trailer
Step-by-step packet journey explanation
Store-and-forward mechanism at each router
Independent routing decisions at each hop
Packet reassembly at destination

questions.question: Discuss the advantages and disadvantages of packet switching. Also explain how congestion occurs in packet-switched networks and suggest methods to control it.
keyPoints: Advantages: efficient resource sharing, resilience, scalability, cost-effectiveness
Disadvantages: variable delays, packet loss, complexity, overhead
Congestion causes: bursty traffic, buffer overflow, routing issues
Congestion control: traffic shaping, RED algorithm, QoS mechanisms
Leaky bucket and token bucket algorithms

questions.question: A message of 8000 bits is to be sent over a network with packet size of 1000 bits. Calculate: (i) Number of packets (ii) If propagation delay is 20ms and transmission delay is 10ms per packet, find total delay for store-and-forward switching.
keyPoints: Number of packets = 8000/1000 = 8 packets
Store-and-forward at multiple hops involves cumulative delays
With single router: total = 8 × (10 + 20) = 240ms (transmission for each packet)
Or considering pipeline: first packet reaches in 20ms, rest follow every 10ms
Clear understanding of serial vs parallel packet transmission

questions.question: Compare circuit switching, message switching, and packet switching with respect to setup time, resource allocation, delay, and suitability for different applications.
keyPoints: Circuit switching: dedicated path, high setup time, constant delay, suitable for voice
Message switching: store-and-forward entire message, high delay, suitable for non-real-time
Packet switching: variable size packets, moderate delay, suitable for data
Resource allocation: dedicated vs on-demand
Delay characteristics: circuit - constant, message - high, packet - variable
